releaseIdentityContract.generatedDigestDisposition: computed and carried by compiler outputs; never embedded in an input authority
releaseIdentityContract.publishedReleaseStatus: external publication record over one passing candidate

semanticVersions.language: independent release-time semantic version or null during development
semanticVersions.compiler: independent release-time semantic version or null during development

authorityOrder.meaning: Versioned semantic clauses in source-of-truth.md and the owning chapter section define user-visible language meaning and feature state.
Appendix B defines tokenization, parseability, grammar disambiguation, and reserved syntax; grammar recognition never implies availability without a feature-state row.
ABI rules, object/interface schemas, report schemas, and emitted artifact encodings define externally visible binary and machine-readable contracts.
Checked examples, compiler tests, snapshots, and fixtures are conformance evidence and must be corrected when they disagree with higher authority.
Examples explain the rules but do not create new language semantics.

featureStates.name: implemented
featureStates.meaning: Accepted by the current compiler or emitted by current tooling with tests or snapshots protecting the contract.

featureStates.name: future-version-normative
featureStates.meaning: Chosen for a named future version or target, but the current compiler may reject it or omit it.

featureStates.name: experimental
featureStates.meaning: An available inspection/research surface or planning rule that is not a normative compatibility promise and may change or be removed without migration support.

featureStates.name: reserved
featureStates.meaning: Syntax, names, encodings, or namespaces are held for later use and rejected unless a later version changes their state.

featureStates.name: deprecated
featureStates.meaning: Still accepted or documented for compatibility, but scheduled for replacement or removal.

featureStates.name: removed
featureStates.meaning: No longer part of Wyst; implementations should reject it or treat it as unknown.

compilationPhases.scope: Whole-program callable and call-site ABI products after semantic verification.
compilationPhases.purpose: Convert verified IR call boundaries and typed aggregate facts into Native ABI and foreign ABI lowering obligations without changing source meaning.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Process callables in canonical symbol order and call sites in source order within each callable.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report ABI mismatch, unsupported foreign boundary, and impossible calling-sequence obligations before machine lowering.
compilationPhases.implementationStatus: Target contract; current lowering paths consume callable, type-layout, and backend resource products without changing ABI behavior.

compilationPhases.scope: CLI invocation, project manifest, explicit root-file mode, normalized artifact-owned or environment-owned layout choice, authenticated target-profile contract and extension set, and host/tool facts that are declared build inputs.
compilationPhases.purpose: Freeze the input set for a compilation so later phases never discover source, target, layout, profile-policy, or extension inputs implicitly.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Normalize input paths and manifest entries before any source text is read.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report invalid invocation shape, missing or forbidden kind-specific manifest fields, layout-owner mismatch, unavailable artifact production, and unknown, absent, stale, partial, or incompatible target-profile products.
compilationPhases.implementationStatus: Target contract; synthetic `<build inputs>` display identity remains only as a non-semantic rendering compatibility adapter.

compilationPhases.scope: Functions, labels, extern declarations, callbacks, indirect callable values, and call sites.
compilationPhases.purpose: Classify each callable boundary under the active ABI using finalized type layout, placement, effect, and execution-level facts.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Classify callables by canonical declaration identity and call sites by source order.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report ABI-inexpressible values, illegal ABI override combinations, and boundary mismatches.

compilationPhases.scope: Top-level and item-level compile-time conditionals selected by deterministic constants.
compilationPhases.purpose: Select the active source item set before generic canonicalization and layout-sensitive analysis.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Evaluate selections in canonical source-graph order and keep selected declarations in source order.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report non-boolean selectors, unknown selected names, and cyclic selection dependencies.
compilationPhases.implementationStatus: Target contract; project target selections are explicit BuildTargetFacts consumed by session semantic and IR products.

compilationPhases.scope: Parsed module top-level declarations, imports, visibility, and declaration headers.
compilationPhases.purpose: Assign stable declaration identities before resolving references or checking bodies.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Collect declarations in source-graph order and source order within each module.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report duplicate declarations, invalid visibility placement, and declaration-header conflicts.

compilationPhases.scope: Function and label bodies after name resolution and type checking.
compilationPhases.purpose: Prove ordinary local reads are initialized while preserving explicit raw-storage operations as separate facts.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Visit bodies in canonical declaration order and control-flow edges in source order.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report ordinary reads before initialization and invalid raw-storage intrinsic use.

compilationPhases.scope: Diagnostics already produced by prior phases.
compilationPhases.purpose: Render text, JSON, and LSP diagnostic payloads without changing semantic facts, accepted programs, emitted bytes, or report facts.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Sort diagnostics by primary source span, phase order, and stable diagnostic code.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Preserve diagnostic meaning and schema fields while formatting phase-owned records.

compilationPhases.scope: Semantic effects, authority assertions, trust-boundary facts, denies, and call-effect summaries.
compilationPhases.purpose: Compute the authority summary consumed by ABI classification, IR construction, diagnostics, and explain reports.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Propagate summaries over the canonical call graph with source-order tie breaking.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report denied effects, false effect declarations visible to the compiler, and invalid trust-boundary placement.

compilationPhases.scope: Execution-level requirements and transitions for functions, labels, callbacks, exception vectors, and privileged intrinsics.
compilationPhases.purpose: Assign the execution-level fact used by semantic diagnostics, ABI classification, and IR construction.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Propagate levels over the canonical control-flow and call graph with source-order tie breaking.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report privileged-operation violations, invalid exception returns, and illegal execution-level transitions.

compilationPhases.scope: Post-lowering backend resources, exact-code resources, frame facts, spill facts, register facts, veneers, and code-size facts.
compilationPhases.purpose: Compute the final resource summary after allocation without reclassifying semantic effects or authority.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Summarize functions in canonical symbol order and machine instructions in emitted order.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report exact-code and resource-contract failures after final backend facts are known.

compilationPhases.scope: Generic declarations and explicit generic argument references after compile-time selection; value-argument lists are empty until a future value-parameter feature exists.
compilationPhases.purpose: Produce canonical generic instantiation identities and substitutions before instantiation.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Canonicalize generics by declaration identity and complete normalized type/value argument order.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report invalid bounds, unsupported generic surfaces, and non-canonical substitution requests.

compilationPhases.scope: Reachable concrete generic functions, structs, enums, and enum variants.
compilationPhases.purpose: Create the closed set of concrete declarations that later layout, ABI, and IR phases consume.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Instantiate reachable substitutions in canonical symbol order and preserve source order within expanded bodies.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report instantiation arity, bound, recursion, and representation failures.

compilationPhases.scope: Fully checked concrete declarations and finalized semantic phase products.
compilationPhases.purpose: Build the authoritative IR module from finalized semantic facts without recomputing semantic ownership.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Emit IR declarations in canonical symbol order and operations in source evaluation order.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report impossible IR construction only when finalized semantic products are inconsistent.
compilationPhases.implementationStatus: Target contract; IR construction consumes explicit products through CompilerSession.

compilationPhases.scope: Layout-sensitive compile-time expressions, field offsets, sizes, alignments, and layout assertions.
compilationPhases.purpose: Evaluate layout-time values after generic instantiation and before type-layout ownership is finalized.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Evaluate layout-time requests by declaration identity and dependency depth with lexical tie breaking.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report non-deterministic layout-time expressions, unsupported layout queries, and layout-time cycles.
compilationPhases.implementationStatus: Target contract; BuildTargetFacts feed layout-time semantic entry points explicitly, while checked layout semantics and type-layout facts are carried by named products instead of ordinary sema::Program symbols.

compilationPhases.scope: Verified IR functions, ABI obligations, scheduling regions, and target instruction selection.
compilationPhases.purpose: Lower IR into target machine operations while preserving finalized semantic and ABI obligations.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Lower functions in canonical symbol order and instructions in IR order subject to explicit scheduling rules.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report target instruction-selection failures and scheduling-contract violations.

compilationPhases.scope: Generated manifest, release evidence, benchmark metadata, and build provenance payloads.
compilationPhases.purpose: Render terminal provenance facts from frozen build inputs and source graph facts without feeding back into semantic or codegen phases.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Render provenance in schema-defined key order with modules and artifacts in canonical order.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report provenance rendering failures without changing build acceptance or artifact bytes.

compilationPhases.scope: Identifiers, paths, imports, type names, value names, callable names, and module-qualified references.
compilationPhases.purpose: Bind references to declaration identities before type checking and constant evaluation.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Resolve modules in source-graph order and references in source order within each declaration.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report unknown names, ambiguous names, visibility violations, and illegal import cycles that escape source-graph ownership.

compilationPhases.scope: Ordinary compile-time constants that are not layout-time facts.
compilationPhases.purpose: Evaluate deterministic constant values used by type checking, compile-time selection, generics, and IR construction.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Evaluate constants by dependency order with declaration identity tie breaking.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report non-constant operations, overflow or domain diagnostics required by constant rules, and constant cycles.

compilationPhases.scope: Source documents after acquisition and lexical classification.
compilationPhases.purpose: Build concrete syntax and module AST products without performing semantic availability or feature-state inference.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Parse source documents in normalized input order and preserve token source order.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report lexical and grammar errors only; semantic feature availability belongs to later phases.

compilationPhases.scope: Section, symbol, object-placement, alignment, and layout-file constraints before concrete placement solving.
compilationPhases.purpose: Collect placement obligations from finalized type layout, target selection, layout file, and source attributes.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Normalize regions, sections, fixed-address claims, and section contributions in canonical declaration and layout-file order while preserving all explicit after edges.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report malformed placement obligations, impossible alignments, duplicate or conflicting placement claims, and unsatisfied normalized constraint inputs.

compilationPhases.scope: Concrete addresses, section offsets, symbol addresses, and placement-dependent facts.
compilationPhases.purpose: Solve placement constraints into stable addresses consumed by ABI classification, IR construction, and artifact preparation.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Solve the normalized placement graph with Chapter 4 dependency order, layout-file order, built-in canonical section order, and bytewise section-name tie breaking.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report dependency cycles, conflicting fixed addresses, inherited-region conflicts, overlaps, alignment conflicts, address overflow, and unsatisfied placement dependencies with the required constraint path.

compilationPhases.scope: Machine operations, ABI register obligations, pinned registers, spills, reloads, and stack-slot allocation.
compilationPhases.purpose: Assign physical registers and stack resources without changing semantic effects or ABI classifications.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Allocate functions in canonical symbol order and virtual registers in first-use order.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report unsatisfied pinning, impossible register class constraints, and allocation failures.

compilationPhases.scope: Object sections, symbols, relocations, debug sections, and final emitted artifact records.
compilationPhases.purpose: Prepare artifact records from allocated machine code and solved placement facts without creating new semantic facts.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Emit sections, symbols, relocations, and debug records in the order defined by Chapter 16 and Chapter 23.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report artifact encoding, relocation, and debug-record preparation failures.

compilationPhases.scope: Explain-report, generated-report, benchmark, and inspection facts that are not consumed by semantic, ABI, lowering, object, or diagnostic decisions.
compilationPhases.purpose: Compute typed terminal report-only summaries from already-owned products without changing accepted programs, emitted bytes, or diagnostics.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Render report facts in schema-defined order with canonical symbol and source-span ordering.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report report-construction failures only; do not reinterpret semantic diagnostics.
compilationPhases.implementationStatus: Current contract; each report kind has a closed typed constructor, records producer/input-product identities, and does not feed compilation products.

compilationPhases.scope: Cross-phase semantic products before ABI lowering and machine lowering.
compilationPhases.purpose: Verify that finalized semantic facts and IR agree before backend phases consume them.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Verify products in canonical fact-owner order and declaration identity order.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report inconsistent phase products and verifier failures before any backend phase may continue.

compilationPhases.scope: Source files, generated source inputs already declared by the build input phase, and immutable source text.
compilationPhases.purpose: Read and fingerprint the frozen input set without discovering additional modules or target facts.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Read sources in normalized build-input order and fingerprint raw UTF-8 bytes.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report unreadable source files, duplicate source identities, and invalid UTF-8 source text.

compilationPhases.scope: Module identities, import graph, root modules, source-to-module mapping, and original-file source-map segments.
compilationPhases.purpose: Construct the authoritative module graph, stable module identities, and source map consumed by declaration collection, diagnostics, and reports.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Traverse the import closure breadth-first; for each module, enumerate the anchor directory's regular non-hidden `.wyst` part files after removing build-owned ignored paths, then sort by normalized project-relative path.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report missing modules, ambiguous modules, import cycles, duplicate module declarations, module-name mismatches, and source graph incompatibilities.

compilationPhases.scope: Resolved declarations, expressions, statements, types, and source attributes before layout-sensitive evaluation.
compilationPhases.purpose: Assign and validate source types while leaving layout, placement, ABI, and report-only facts to their owning phases.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Check declarations in dependency order with declaration identity tie breaking and source-order body traversal.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report type mismatches, invalid operations, invalid attributes, and unsupported type surfaces.
compilationPhases.implementationStatus: Target contract; BuildTargetFacts feed semantic entry points explicitly.

compilationPhases.scope: Concrete scalar, aggregate, enum, bitstruct, array, slice, descriptor, and address layouts.
compilationPhases.purpose: Finalize the authoritative type-layout facts consumed by placement, ABI classification, IR construction, debug info, and reports.
compilationPhases.deterministicOrderingRule: Lay out types by dependency order with canonical declaration identity tie breaking.
compilationPhases.diagnosticResponsibility: Report recursive unsized layout, invalid representation, impossible alignment, and layout assertion failures.
compilationPhases.implementationStatus: Target contract; type-layout facts are carried by TypeLayoutFacts products and extracted checked-layout facts rather than ordinary sema::Program symbols.

semanticFactOwners.meaning: Immutable acquired source document bytes and identity before parsing.
Target, normalized layout owner/choice, complete profile-policy tuple, authenticated contract/extension schemas and digests, and declared build facts frozen before source acquisition.
Parsed module syntax tree and token stream facts produced before semantic selection.
Selected AST after active compile-time branches are chosen.
Instantiated AST after current generic expansion and statement-level compile-time selection.
Stable module identity and import-graph membership for a frozen source graph.
Original-file identity and normalized project-relative mapping for every byte span in the current-build source product.
Stable identity for declarations before reference resolution.
Binding from a source reference to its owning declaration identity.
Checked source type for declarations, expressions, statements, and constant expressions.
Deterministic non-layout compile-time constant value.
Deterministic value that depends on finalized generic/type-layout context.
Authoritative size, alignment, field offset, enum representation, and descriptor layout facts.
Concrete placement address, section offset, or symbol-address fact.
Semantic effect, authority, trust-boundary, and deny-policy summary.
Execution-level requirement or transition fact for callable and control-flow boundaries.
Native or foreign ABI classification for callable boundaries and call sites.
Allocation-free callable ABI obligations projected for machine lowering.
Authoritative typed IR module built from finalized semantic facts.
Target machine operations and emitted machine-code facts before final artifact preparation.
Assigned register homes, stack homes, and allocation summary facts.
Final backend resource summary after lowering and allocation.
Prepared artifact bytes, sections, symbols, relocations, and debug records.
Terminal report-only summary that cannot feed semantic, ABI, lowering, object, diagnostic, or acceptance decisions.

behaviorCategories.name: Defined
behaviorCategories.meaning: Wyst specifies the exact compile-time diagnostic or runtime result.

behaviorCategories.name: Target-defined
behaviorCategories.meaning: The selected target descriptor, ABI profile, or architecture manual specifies the result.

behaviorCategories.name: Indeterminate bits
behaviorCategories.meaning: An unspecified machine bit pattern is observed as an ordinary typed value; it is never optimizer poison or undef.

behaviorCategories.name: Architectural fault or trap
behaviorCategories.meaning: The emitted operation may synchronously fault, trap, or complete according to the selected architecture and current machine state.

behaviorCategories.name: Trusted-contract violation
behaviorCategories.meaning: The program supplied a false ABI, assembly, address, function-pointer, or foreign-interface assertion.

behaviorClassifications.summary: Signed and unsigned integer overflow wraps at the type width.
x / 0 produces 0; x % 0 and x %% 0 produce x.
The quotient wraps to the input value and the remainder is 0.
Unsigned shift counts are reduced modulo max(32, width(T)); signed counts are rejected.
Floating-point arithmetic follows IEEE 754 results for exceptional cases.
The selected architecture's conversion instruction defines out-of-range, NaN, and infinity conversion results.
Loads and stores through unmapped, stale, non-canonical, or otherwise invalid data addresses may fault or complete according to machine state.
Address 0 is an ordinary address value; accessing it may fault or complete according to the active target mapping.

AArch64 ordinary and volatile 16-, 32-, and 64-bit accesses admit unaligned addresses; MMIO requires natural alignment, rejects provable misalignment, and classifies dynamic misalignment as a possible architectural fault or trap.
Misaligned pair, vector, exclusive, or non-temporal access may fault according to the architecture.
Invalid source-level goto and malformed label transfers are rejected before emission.
Function-pointer arithmetic, ordered comparison, memory access, and visible convention mismatches are rejected before emission.
A false trusted_callable<T>(address) address, signature, or ABI assertion is the program's contract violation.
An ordinary read of a local before initialization is rejected before emission.
MaybeUninit<T>.read_uninit() observes raw storage as ordinary typed bits with unspecified contents and leaves initialization evidence unchanged.
An access-atomic racing load observes a value permitted by the selected architecture's memory model.
A non-access-atomic racing load may observe a bit pattern assembled from independently racing sub-accesses.
Foreign ABI mismatches visible in Wyst declarations or function pointer types are rejected before emission.
A false foreign declaration, C header fact, object symbol assertion, or variadic ABI assertion is the program's contract violation.
Unsupported inline assembly mnemonics, operands, branch targets, clobbers, or contradictory effects are rejected before emission.
A missing clobber, false effect declaration, or false pure assertion not provable by the compiler is the program's contract violation.
Raw memory reads of inactive enum payload bytes observe ordinary typed bits with unspecified contents.

a64Conformance.claim: complete conformance for the exact focused active support profile; universal A64 conformance is not claimed

bitstructContract.declarationShape: bitstruct Name: Backing { FIELD: Carrier at Location ... }

declarations.registerMap: register_map Name { REGISTER: ACCESS Backing at Offset FieldBlock? ... }
declarations.scalarMmio: mmio NAME: ACCESS T at Address
declarations.encodedSystemRegister: system_register NAME: ACCESS u64 at SystemRegisterEncoding HardwareFieldBlock?

declarationAttributeCatalog.activationRule: an owning semantic contract atomically installs an implemented state, signature, subjects, conflicts, formatter order, tooling metadata, and behavior

genericBoundCatalog.extensionRule: an owning language semantic contract adds one complete active subject-and-capability row before the bound is accepted

stackTransition.sourceShape: asm establishes stack (stack: u64 in x1 = VALUE) { mov sp, stack }

authenticatedVariants.selectedRootShape: pub naked fn _start(dtb: @u8 in x0) -> never

stackTransition.sourceShape: asm establishes stack (stack: u64 in x1 = VALUE) { mov sp, stack }

reportSchemas.producer: wync explain lowering --format json and text header
wync explain effects --format json and text header
wync explain storage --format json and text header

interfaceSchemas.producer: generic release nomination, gate, and publication tooling
